attacks.name: Hydro Reflect
attacks.effect: You may move all Energy cards attached to Palkia to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. (Ignore this effect if you don't have any Benched Pokémon.)

rules: Put this card onto your Active Palkia. Palkia LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

attacks.name: Metal Flash
attacks.effect: During your next turn, Dialga can't use Metal Flash.

rules: Put this card onto your Active Dialga. Dialga LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

attacks.name: Endless Darkness
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Flip 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, the Defending Pokémon is still Asleep. If both of them are tails, the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out.

rules: Put this card onto your Active Darkrai. Darkrai LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

attacks.name: Moon Skip
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out by this attack, take 1 more Prize card.

rules: Put this card onto your Active Cresselia. Cresselia LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.
Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 1 or Stage 2 card that evolves from that Pokémon in your hand, put that card on the Basic Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.)
Search your deck or your discard pile for a Pokémon LV.X, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. If you search your deck, shuffle your deck afterward.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

The Retreat Cost for each Psychic and Darkness Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) is 0.

Attach Leftovers to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

If the Pokémon Leftovers is attached to is your Active Pokémon at the end of your turn, remove 1 damage counter from the Pokémon.

You can play only one Supporter card each turn. When you play this card, put it next to your Active Pokémon. When your turn ends, discard this card.

Discard up to 2 cards from your hand. If you discard 1 card, draw 3 cards. If you discard 2 cards, draw 4 cards.

Attach Amulet Coin to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it. If that Pokémon is Knocked Out, discard this card.

If the Pokémon Amulet Coin is attached to is your Active Pokémon at the end of your turn, draw a card.

attacks.name: Pull Out
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for any 1 card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of your deck.

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It walks in zigzag fashion. It is good at finding items in the grass and even in the ground.
It makes its nest on steep sea cliffs. Riding updrafts, it soars to great heights.

attacks.name: Uproar
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Usually, its cries are like quiet murmurs.  If frightened, it shrieks at the same volume as a jet plane.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It eats its weight in leaves every day. It fends off attackers with the needle on its head.

attacks.name: Firefly Light
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It communicates with others by lighting up its rear at night. It loves ILLUMISE's sweet aroma.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.effect: Search either player's discard pile for up to any 2 cards, show them to your opponent, and put them on top of that player's deck in any order you like.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

attacks.name: Energy Ball
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Treecko but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.effect: Remove 1 damage counter from Treecko.

flavorText: The soles of its feet are covered by countless tiny spikes, enabling it to walk on walls and ceilings.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: A fire burns inside, so it feels very warm to hug. It launches fireballs of 1,800 degrees F.

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Its shell is said to be stuffed with happiness that it shares with kindhearted people.

attacks.name: Vine Invite
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Grass Knot
attacks.effect: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Colorless Energy in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the the Retreat Cost).

flavorText: It is shrouded by blue vines. No one has seen the face hidden behind this growth of vines.

attacks.name: Super Speed
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Swablu during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Shoot Through
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its wings are like cotton tufts. If it perches on someone's head, it looks like a cotton hat.

attacks.name: Sol Charge
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: A new Pokémon species, rumored to be from the sun. It gives off light while spinning.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Small Pokémon flee from its scary face. It is, however, considered by women to be cute.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its body is made of magma. If it doesn't keep moving, its body will cool and harden.

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.effect: Remove all Special Conditions and 3 damage counters from Slowpoke. Slowpoke is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Tumble Over
attacks.effect: Slowpoke can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: Although slow, it is skilled at fishing with its tail. It does not feel pain if its tail is bitten.

attacks.name: Calculate
attacks.effect: Look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

flavorText: The world's first artificially created Pokémon. It can travel through electronic space.

attacks.name: Endeavor
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.effect: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Mankey.

flavorText: It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

attacks.name: Arm Thrust
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It toughens its body by slamming into thick trees. Many snapped trees can be found near its nest.

attacks.name: Rendezvous
attacks.effect: Reveal the top card of your deck and put it into your hand. If that card is a Pokémon, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Synchro Dance
attacks.effect: If Luvdisc and the Defending Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in warm seas. It is said that a couple finding this Pokémon will be blessed with eternal love.

attacks.name: Knock Over
attacks.effect: You may discard any Stadium card in play.

flavorText: Because it turns active on nights of the full moon, it is said to have some link to the lunar phases.
It lives in burrows dug on sandy beaches. Its pincers fully grow back if they are broken in battle.

attacks.name: Smogscreen
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Lighter-than-air gases in its body keep it aloft. The gases not only smell, they are also explosive.

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Kakuna by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Jigglypuff by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When it wavers its big, round eyes, it begins singing a lullaby that makes everyone drowsy.

attacks.name: Firefly Scent
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has any Poké-Bodies, that Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: With its sweet aroma, it guides VOLBEAT to draw signs with light in the night sky.

attacks.name: Inquire
attacks.effect: Draw a card. If you didn't play any Supporter card from your hand during this turn, draw 2 more cards.

flavorText: It has a soft and bouncy body. Once it starts bouncing, it becomes impossible to stop.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Houndour.

flavorText: It conveys its feelings using different cries. It works in a pack to cleverly take down prey.

attacks.name: Captivate
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate. Its fickleness is very popular among some.

attacks.name: Fast Evolution
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It is famous for its shabby appearance. While populous, they tend to cluster in set locations.

attacks.name: Shed Skin
attacks.effect: Remove 2 damage counters from Ekans.

attacks.name: Poison Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It sneaks through grass without making a sound and strikes unsuspecting prey from behind.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It can tell what people are dreaming by sniffing with its big nose. It loves fun dreams.

attacks.name: Clamp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It makes a single pearl during its lifetime. The pearl is said to amplify psychic power.
It releases a stench from its red antenna to repel enemies. It grows by molting repeatedly.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: By storing water in its body, this desert dweller can survive 30 days without water.
It has a flotation sac that is like an inflatable collar. It floats on water with its head out.

attacks.name: Psychic Balance
attacks.effect: If you have less cards in your hand than your opponent, draw cards until you have the same number of cards as your opponent. (If you have more or the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does nothing.)

attacks.name: Spin Turn
attacks.effect: Switch Baltoy with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It moves by spinning on its foot. It is a rare Pokémon that was discovered in ancient ruins.

attacks.name: Cutting Claw
attacks.effect: If your opponent has Seviper in play, this attack does 40 damage plus 50 more damage.

flavorText: It has feuded with SEVIPER for many generations. Its sharp claws are its biggest weapons.

attacks.name: Tsunami
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: On sunny days, it lands on beaches to bounce like a ball and play. It spouts water from its nose.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.effect: If Unown G has any damage counters on it, this attack's base damage is 10.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.effect: You may flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, this attack's base damage is 30.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

attacks.name: Sweet Kiss
attacks.effect: Your opponent may draw a card.

flavorText: It is said to appear in front of kindly people to scatter a glowing down called "joy dust."

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Though usually dim witted, it seems to become inspired if the SHELLDER on its tail bites down.

attacks.name: Air Crash
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Skarmory by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Despite being clad entirely in iron-hard armor, it flies at speeds over 180 mph.

attacks.name: Tail Blade
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play any Trainer cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Poison Tail
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: For many generations, it has feuded with ZANGOOSE. It whets its bladed tail on rocks for battle.

attacks.name: Primal Calling
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Trainer Cards that have Fossil in their names, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Sleep Swirl
attacks.effect: Both Relicanth and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.

flavorText: A rare Pokémon discovered during a deep-sea exploration. It has not changed in over 100 million years.

attacks.name: Swagger
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is a brazen brute that barges its way into another Pokémon's nest and claims it as its own.
With planetary development software installed, it became capable of working in space.

attacks.name: Guzzle
attacks.effect: Choose up to 2 basic Water Energy cards from your hand and attach them to Pelipper. Remove 2 damage counters for each Energy card attached in this way.

attacks.name: Jet Return
attacks.effect: Does 30 times the number of Energy cards attached to Pelipper. Then, put all Energy cards attached to Pelipper on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It dips its large bill in the sea, then scoops up numerous prey along with water.
A steel-hard shell protects its tender body. It quietly endures hardships while awaiting evolution.

attacks.name: Muddy Bomb
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon and 10 damage to each of your opponent's other Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its sturdy legs give it sure footing, even in mud. It burrows into dirt to sleep.

attacks.name: Lava Plume
attacks.effect: You may discard the top card of your deck. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Its body temperature is roughly 18,000 degrees F. Flames spout from gaps in its hardened shell.

attacks.name: Bass Control
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: The shock waves from its cries can tip over trucks. It stamps its feet to power up.

attacks.name: Switcheroo
attacks.effect: Move a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon (excluding Pokémon that already has a Pokémon Tool attached to it). (If an effect of this attack is prevented, this attack does nothing.)

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It charges prey at speeds over 60 mph. However, because it can only run straight, it often fails.

attacks.name: Scary Face
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack or retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Threaten and Drop
attacks.effect: If Huntail has any Darkness Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage and discard a Special Energy card, if any, attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives deep in the sea. With a tail shaped like a small fish, it attracts unsuspecting prey.

attacks.name: Palm Strike
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Spirited Throw
attacks.effect: If Hariyama has fewer remaining HP than the Defending Pokémon, this attack's base damage is 80.

flavorText: It loves to match power with big-bodied Pokémon. It can knock a truck flying with its arm thrusts.

attacks.name: Greenness
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Grass Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Poison Breath
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It lives in dense jungles. While closing in on its prey, it leaps from branch to branch.

attacks.name: Daunt
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.effect: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Granbull.

flavorText: It is timid in spite of its looks. If it becomes enraged, however, it will strike with its huge fangs.

attacks.name: Sweet Temptation
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to the new Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic Snap
attacks.effect: If Gorebyss has any Psychic Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It lives at the bottom of the sea. In the springtime, its pink body turns more vivid for some reason.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Floatzel during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Aqua Jet
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It floats using its well-developed floatation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.

attacks.name: Present
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It carries food rolled up in its tail. It has the habit of sharing food with people lost in mountains.

attacks.name: Bulk Up
attacks.effect: During your next turn, each of Combusken's attacks does 30 more damage to the Defending Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its kicking mastery lets it loose 10 kicks per second. It emits sharp cries to intimidate foes.

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Needle Arm
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It becomes active at night, seeking prey that is exhausted from the day's desert heat.

attacks.name: Deadly Poison
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 10 damage plus 40 more damage. If the Defending Pokémon is not Poisoned, that Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The pattern on its belly is for intimidation. It constricts foes while they are frozen in fear.

attacks.name: Ballon Attack
attacks.effect: If Igglybuff is anywhere under Wigglytuff, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its fine fur feels sublime to the touch. It can expand its body by inhaling air.

attacks.name: Offensive Gas
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

flavorText: It grows by feeding on gases released by garbage. Though very rare, triplets have been found.

attacks.name: Sink Deep
attacks.effect: If Wailord has any damage counters on it, Wailord is now Asleep.

flavorText: The biggest of all Pokémon. It can dive to a depth of almost 10,000 feet on only one breath.

attacks.name: Hidden Power
attacks.effect: Discard a card from your hand. (If you can't discard a card from your hand, this attack does nothing.)

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or UNOWN came first.

attacks.name: Psych Up
attacks.effect: During your next turn, Slowking's Psych Up attack's base damage is 60.

flavorText: Being bitten by Shellder gave it intelligence comparable to that of award-winning scientists.

attacks.name: Whump
attacks.effect: Primeape is now Confused. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It grows angry if you see its eyes and gets angrier if you run. If you beat it, it gets even madder.

attacks.name: Spacial Rend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Stadium card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. If there is any Stadium card in play, discard it.

attacks.name: Transback
attacks.effect: You may flip a coin. If heads, discard all Energy attached to Palkia and put the Defending Pokémon and all cards attached to it on top of your opponent's deck. Your opponent shuffles his or her deck afterward.

flavorText: It has the ability to distort space. It is described as a deity in Sinnoh-region mythology.

attacks.name: Twister
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If both are tails, this attack does nothing. For each heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: When people bicker, it is said to arise from the depths of lakes to becalm violent hearts.

attacks.name: Pick Out
attacks.effect: Choose 1 face-down Prize card (yours or your opponent's) and put it face up. If that card is a Supporter card, use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack. (That card remains face up for the rest of the game.)

attacks.name: Jaw Bite
attacks.effect: During your next turn, if an attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Attached to its head is a huge set of jaws formed by horns. It can chew through iron beams.

attacks.name: Energy Draw
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to Latios. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Luster Purge
attacks.effect: Discard 3 Energy attached to Latios.

flavorText: A highly intelligent Pokémon. By folding back its wings in flight, it can overtake jet planes.

attacks.name: Miraculous Light
attacks.effect: Remove 2 damage counters and all Special Conditions from Latias.

attacks.name: Mist Ball
attacks.effect: Discard a Fire Energy and a Water Energy attached to Latias.

flavorText: Its body is covered with a down that can refract light in such a way that it becomes invisible.

attacks.name: Carry In
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Pokémon Tool Card, a Supporter card, and a basic Energy card, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It loves crossing the sea with people and Pokémon on its back. It understands human speech.

attacks.name: Scissor Blow
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon would be be Knocked Out by this attack, Kingler does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: The larger pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, it is so heavy, it is difficult to aim.

attacks.name: Dream Catcher
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep, move all damage counters from Hypno to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Seeing its swinging pendulum can induce sleep in three seconds, even if someone just woke up.

attacks.name: Dark Fang
attacks.effect: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.

attacks.name: Black Fire
attacks.effect: You may discard a Fire or Darkness Energy attached to Houndoom. If you discard a Fire Energy, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned. If you discard a Darkness Energy, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: Long ago, people imagined its eerie howls to be the call of the grim reaper.

attacks.name: Ambient Noise
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. If the first coin is heads, this attack does 50 damage to the Defending Pokémon. If the first coin is tails, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If the second coin is heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If the second coin is tails, your opponent can't play any Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its howls can be heard over six miles away. It emits all sorts of noises from the ports on its body.

attacks.name: Time Bellow
attacks.effect: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Flash Cannon
attacks.effect: You may return all Energy cards attached to Dialga to your hand. If you do, remove the highest Stage Evolution card from the Defending Pokémon and shuffle that card into your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.
An ancient clay figurine that came to life as a Pokémon from exposure to a mysterious ray of light.

attacks.name: Parallel Drain
attacks.effect: After your attack, remove from 1 of your Pokémon the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dozing Scales
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It loves the honey of flowers and can locate flower patches that have even tiny amounts of pollen.

attacks.name: Band Attack
attacks.effect: Does 30 damage times the number of Beedrill you have in play.

attacks.name: Twineedle
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its best attack involves flying around at high speed, striking with poison needles, then flying off.

attacks.name: Dragonbreath
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Cotton Cloud
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Altaria by attacks from your opponent's Evolved Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It looks like a fluffy cloud when it is in flight. It hums with its soprano voice.

attacks.name: Air Scroll
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage. If tails, remove 3 damage counters from Togekiss.

flavorText: It will never appear where there is strife. Its sightings have become rare recently.

attacks.name: Power Whip
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage for each basic Energy card attached to Tangrowth to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Stick and Absorb
attacks.effect: Remove 3 damage counters from Tangrowth. The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.

attacks.name: Hammer Arm
attacks.effect: Discard the top card from your opponent's deck.

flavorText: It can swim while towing a large ship. It bashes down foes with a swing of its thick arms.

attacks.name: Leaf Blade
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: The leaves that grow on its arms can slice down thick trees. It is without peer in jungle combat.

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.effect: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Telebeam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

attacks.name: Tri Attack
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Additional software was installed to make it a better Pokémon. It began acting oddly, however.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Smash Short
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, this attack does 10 damage plus 30 more damage. Discard that Pokémon Tool card, look at your opponent's hand, and discard any Pokémon Tool cards of the same name you find there.

flavorText: It makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

attacks.name: Darkness Roar
attacks.effect: You may have your opponent switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 or his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sleep Hole
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 40 damage plus 40 more damage. If the Defending Pokémon is not Asleep, that Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

attacks.name: Reach Over
attacks.effect: Search your deck for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Darkness Pursuit
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is Cresselia, this attack's base damage is 100.

flavorText: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

attacks.name: Moon Twinkle
attacks.effect: If there is any Stadium card in play, remove 2 damage counters from Cresselia.

attacks.name: Lunar Dance
attacks.effect: You may discard 2 Energy attached to Cresselia. If you do, remove all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

attacks.name: Fire Dance
attacks.effect: Search your discard pile for a Fire Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Flame Kick
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Fire Energy attached to Blaziken. This attack does 80 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Flames spout from its wrists, enveloping its knuckles. Its punches scorch its foes.
